mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in industrial production expands manufacturing output, which raises corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 50% of months when INDPRO increases month-over-month during the 2000-2005 period.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window reflects deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending forecasts and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 55% of instances when the trigger fires, or the mean return is not statistically below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in consumer prices and the federal funds rate generate a stagflationary monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and lower real earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising CPIAUCSL and rising FEDFUNDS does not produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) over individual effects.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a month-over-month decline in housing starts reflects weakening residential investment that drags aggregate demand lower, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods classified as late-cycle expansion, declines in HOUST are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, or the signal's Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls constitute a broad collapse in consumer and income-driven demand that reduces revenue expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint occurrence of declining RSAFS and declining PAYEMS fails to produce negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or either series alone explains the returns equally well without interaction.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent reflects restrictive monetary policy that elevates borrowing costs and discount rates, suppressing equity prices and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50% of months, or the mean return is not below the full-sample mean (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales directly captures consumer spending momentum that lifts near-term revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations, or the Rank IC over a 2-year rolling window falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in industrial production reflects contracting manufacturing activity that immediately reduces earnings revisions and risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances, or the hit rate of negative returns is not statistically different from 50% (binomial p > 0.05).
